zapier alternatives github — Complete Guide [2026]

AI AI Tools Idea · May 23, 2026 · Updated May 23, 2026
— min read
Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. Learn more →
🚀 AI Platform

As a small business owner, you’re likely struggling to find the perfect automation tool to streamline your workflow, and you’re considering zapier alternatives on GitHub to help you achieve your goals. With so many options available, it can be overwhelming to decide which one is right for you. In this article, we’ll explore the best Zapier alternatives on GitHub, including their features, pricing, and pros and cons.

Zapier is a popular automation tool that allows you to connect different apps and services to automate tasks and workflows. However, it may not be the best fit for every business, especially those on a tight budget or with specific integration requirements. Try Zapier free to see if it’s right for you, but if you’re looking for alternatives, keep reading.

Here’s a comparison table of Zapier and some of its alternatives on GitHub:

ToolPricingFeaturesIntegrations
Zapier$19.99/month (Starter)Automated workflows, multi-step zaps, filters1,000+ apps
AutomatorFree (open-source)Automated workflows, conditional logic, webhooks100+ apps
n8nFree (open-source)Automated workflows, conditional logic, webhooks200+ apps
HuginnFree (open-source)Automated workflows, agents, webhooks50+ apps
IFTTTFree (basic), $3.99/month (Pro)Automated applets, conditional logic, webhooks600+ apps

As you can see, there are many alternatives to Zapier on GitHub, each with its own strengths and weaknesses. Some of the key reasons why someone might switch from Zapier to an alternative include:

  • Cost: Zapier can be expensive, especially for small businesses or individuals. Many of the alternatives on GitHub are free or open-source, making them a more affordable option.
  • Customization: Zapier has a limited number of integrations and features, which may not be enough for businesses with complex workflows. Some alternatives, like n8n and Automator, offer more advanced features and customization options.
  • Security: Some businesses may require more control over their data and workflows, which can be a concern with Zapier. Alternatives like Huginn and IFTTT offer more flexibility and security features.

Pro tip: When evaluating Zapier alternatives, consider the number of integrations and features you need. If you only require a few simple automations, a free or low-cost option like IFTTT or Automator might be sufficient. However, if you need more advanced features or a large number of integrations, a paid option like Zapier or n8n might be a better choice.

Let’s take a closer look at some of the free and paid Zapier alternatives on GitHub:

Free Options:

  • Automator: Automator is a free, open-source automation tool that allows you to create custom workflows with conditional logic and webhooks. It has a user-friendly interface and supports over 100 apps, including popular services like Google Drive and Trello.
  • n8n: n8n is another free, open-source automation tool that offers advanced features like conditional logic and webhooks. It has a large community of users and supports over 200 apps, including GitHub and Slack.
  • Huginn: Huginn is a free, open-source automation tool that allows you to create custom agents and workflows. It has a steep learning curve, but offers advanced features like webhooks and conditional logic.

Paid Options:

  • IFTTT Pro: IFTTT Pro is a paid version of the popular automation tool IFTTT. It offers additional features like multi-step applets and conditional logic, as well as priority support. Pricing starts at $3.99/month.
  • Zapier: Zapier is a paid automation tool that offers a wide range of features and integrations. Pricing starts at $19.99/month for the Starter plan, which includes 100 tasks per month and support for 1,000+ apps. Try Zapier free to see if it’s right for you.

Pro tip: When choosing a paid Zapier alternative, consider the cost per task or automation. Some tools, like Zapier, charge per task, while others, like IFTTT Pro, charge a flat monthly fee. Make sure to calculate the total cost of ownership and choose the option that best fits your budget.

Pro tip: Don’t forget to evaluate the customer support and community resources available for each tool. A strong community and good support can make a big difference in your success with automation.

In addition to the tools mentioned above, there are many other Zapier alternatives on GitHub that are worth considering. Some popular options include:

  • Zapier API: Zapier API is a free, open-source alternative to Zapier that allows you to create custom integrations and workflows. It has a steep learning curve, but offers advanced features like webhooks and conditional logic.
  • Microsoft Power Automate: Microsoft Power Automate is a paid automation tool that offers a wide range of features and integrations. Pricing starts at $15/month for the Starter plan, which includes 100 tasks per month and support for 200+ apps.

As of 2026, the pricing for these tools is as follows:

  • Zapier: $19.99/month (Starter), $49/month (Pro), $299/month (Team)
  • IFTTT Pro: $3.99/month
  • n8n: free (open-source)
  • Automator: free (open-source)
  • Huginn: free (open-source)

In conclusion, there are many Zapier alternatives on GitHub that offer a range of features and pricing options. By considering your specific needs and budget, you can choose the best tool for your business and start automating your workflows today. Try Zapier free to see if it’s right for you, or explore the other options mentioned in this article.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best Zapier alternative for small businesses?

The best Zapier alternative for small businesses depends on your specific needs and budget. If you’re looking for a free or low-cost option, Automator or IFTTT might be a good choice. However, if you need more advanced features or a large number of integrations, a paid option like Zapier or n8n might be a better fit.

Can I use Zapier alternatives for personal projects?

Yes, many Zapier alternatives on GitHub are suitable for personal projects. In fact, some tools, like IFTTT and Automator, are designed specifically for personal use and offer a user-friendly interface and a wide range of integrations.

How do I choose the right Zapier alternative for my business?

To choose the right Zapier alternative for your business, consider the following factors: the number of integrations you need, the features you require, and your budget. You should also evaluate the customer support and community resources available for each tool, as well as the pricing model and cost per task or automation.

Are Zapier alternatives secure?

Yes, many Zapier alternatives on GitHub offer advanced security features, such as encryption and two-factor authentication. However, it’s always important to evaluate the security features of any tool before using it for your business, and to follow best practices for data protection and security.
